perturbation.original_text: 4. Except as provided in Paragraphs 6 and 7 hereof, Proprietary Information disclosed to a receiving Party shall for a period of three (3 ) years from the effective date of thi s Agreement be held in confidence by the receiving Party and not be disclosed to others or used except for the purposes set forth above, without the prior written approval of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Except as provided in Paragraphs 6 and 7 hereof, Proprietary Information disclosed to a receiving Party shall be held in confidence by the receiving Party and not be disclosed to others or used except for the purposes set forth above, without the prior written approval of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'for a period of three (3) years from the effective date of this Agreement', the duration of confidentiality is undefined, contradicting the termination clauses in Section 10, which references a specific period for data protection. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 10 relies on the definition of the confidentiality period established in Section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement shall be e ffective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate at the end of the period of years as provided for in paragraph 4 above, or upon the delivery of written notice of termination by a Party to the other Party; however, the obligations of a receiving Party pursuant to Paragraph 4 shall remain in effect for the term specified therein. b. Notwithstanding the termination of this agreement, the supplied data must be maintained and protected in accordance with its provisions fo r three (3) years following the termination of this agreement. At the conclusion of this agreement/contract for which data is exchanged, proprietary data shall be returned to the provider or destroyed with a certification to that effect provided to the ot her party, except the one (1) copy retained by legal counsel as provided in paragraph 8 above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4 removes the specified duration of 'three (3) years' for confidentiality, creating an in-text contradiction with Section 10. Section 10a references the period provided in paragraph 4 for agreement termination, and Section 10b requires data to be protected for three years following the agreement's termination, implicitly relying on the term established in Section 4. By removing the explicit period in Section 4, the termination clauses in Section 10 become ambiguous and potentially unenforceable, thus a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Upon written request of a Party who has disclosed Proprietary Information to a receiving Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return all Proprietary Information except that one copy may be retained by legal counsel of the receiving Party as evidence of what was disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Upon written request of a Party who has disclosed Proprietary Information to a receiving Party, the receiving Party shall return all Proprietary Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exception 'except that one copy may be retained by legal counsel of the receiving Party as evidence of what was disclosed,' the contract now mandates the return of *all* Proprietary Information. This contradicts section 10b, which makes an exception for the legal counsel. Thus, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement shall be e ffective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate at the end of the period of years as provided for in paragraph 4 above, or upon the delivery of written notice of termination by a Party to the other Party; however, the obligations of a receiving Party pursuant to Paragraph 4 shall remain in effect for the term specified therein. b. Notwithstanding the termination of this agreement, the supplied data must be maintained and protected in accordance with its provisions fo r three (3) years following the termination of this agreement. At the conclusion of this agreement/contract for which data is exchanged, proprietary data shall be returned to the provider or destroyed with a certification to that effect provided to the ot her party, except the one (1) copy retained by legal counsel as provided in paragraph 8 above.
perturbation.justification: The original text in paragraph 8 allowed legal counsel to retain a copy of Proprietary Information. The changed text removes this exception, requiring the return of all Proprietary Information. This directly contradicts paragraph 10b, which explicitly states an exception for legal counsel retaining one copy of the data, as provided in paragraph 8. The removal of the exception in paragraph 8 directly conflicts with the continued existence of that exception in paragraph 10b, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Each Party acknowledges that the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder may be subject to export control, and that compliance with all appropriate Government regulations such as the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (IT AR), the Export Administration Regulations (EAR), etc., may be necessary to obtain required approvals before disclosing Proprietary Information to foreign nationals, businesses or governments. The receiving Party shall obtain the written consent of the di sclosing Party prior to submitting any request for authority to export any such Proprietary Information. The receiving Party shall indemnify and hold the disclosing Party harmless from all claims, demands, damages, costs, fines, penalties, attorneys’ fees , and all other expenses arising from failure of the receiving Party to comply with this clause or the ITAR or the EAR.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Each Party acknowledges that the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder may be subject to export control, and that compliance with all appropriate Government regulations may be necessary to obtain required approvals before disclosing Proprietary Information to foreign nationals, businesses or governments. The receiving Party shall obtain the written consent of the di sclosing Party prior to submitting any request for authority to export any such Proprietary Information. The receiving Party shall indemnify and hold the disclosing Party harmless from all claims, demands, damages, costs, fines, penalties, attorneys’ fees , and all other expenses arising from failure of the receiving Party to comply with this clause.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'such as the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (IT AR), the Export Administration Regulations (EAR), etc.,' and 'or the ITAR or the EAR' the specificity of compliance with export control regulations is lost. The general compliance requirement remains, but the specific examples and enforcement mechanism are weakened. This weakens the indemnification obligation. The in-text contradiction lies in the fact that the receiving party indemnifies for not complying with this clause but ITAR and EAR no longer exists.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Each Party acknowledges that the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder may be subject to export control, and that compliance with all appropriate Government regulations may be necessary to obtain required approvals before disclosing Proprietary Information to foreign nationals, businesses or governments. The receiving Party shall obtain the written consent of the di sclosing Party prior to submitting any request for authority to export any such Proprietary Information. The receiving Party shall indemnify and hold the disclosing Party harmless from all claims, demands, damages, costs, fines, penalties, attorneys’ fees , and all other expenses arising from failure of the receiving Party to comply with this clause.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific examples of 'International Traffic in Arms Regulations (IT AR), the Export Administration Regulations (EAR), etc.,' from the original text and also removes 'or the ITAR or the EAR' from the indemnification clause. This weakens the indemnification obligation. The explanation states that this creates an in-text contradiction because the receiving party indemnifies for not complying with this clause, but ITAR and EAR no longer exists. The contradicted text is identical to the Changed Text, which shows that it's missing the ITAR/EAR mentions, creating an incompatibility with the intended scope of the original text.

perturbation.original_text: 14. The parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique nature of the Proprietary Information, and breach of this agreement by the receiving party would cause irreparable harm to th e disclosing party and that the disclosing party shall therefore be entitled to equitable relief in addition to all other remedies at law. The parties agree that, in the event of breach, or threatened breach of the terms of this agreement, the originating party may seek an injunction prohibiting such breach. Both parties hereby waiver any requirement to post bond for attempts to obtain such injunctive relief. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of m onetary damages. Each party shall pay its own attorney’s fees, costs, and expenses for any dispute under the agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. The parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique nature of the Proprietary Information, and breach of this agreement by the receiving party would cause irreparable harm to th e disclosing party and that the disclosing party shall therefore be entitled to equitable relief in addition to all other remedies at law. The parties agree that, in the event of breach, or threatened breach of the terms of this agreement, the originating party may seek an injunction prohibiting such breach. Both parties hereby waiver any requirement to post bond for attempts to obtain such injunctive relief. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of m onetary damages.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Each party shall pay its own attorney’s fees, costs, and expenses for any dispute under the agreement', it's unclear if the prevailing party can collect attorney's fees. It introduces uncertainty. Without that clause, the standard rules of civil procedure may apply (where the losing party pays the winner's legal costs) potentially contradicting an initial expectation and increasing litigation risk. Thus, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. The parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique nature of the Proprietary Information, and breach of this agreement by the receiving party would cause irreparable harm to th e disclosing party and that the disclosing party shall therefore be entitled to equitable relief in addition to all other remedies at law. The parties agree that, in the event of breach, or threatened breach of the terms of this agreement, the originating party may seek an injunction prohibiting such breach. Both parties hereby waiver any requirement to post bond for attempts to obtain such injunctive relief. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of m onetary damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that 'Each party shall pay its own attorney’s fees, costs, and expenses for any dispute under the agreement.' The changed text omits this clause. This omission creates a contradiction because it introduces uncertainty regarding which party is responsible for attorney's fees in case of a dispute, potentially conflicting with the implied understanding that each party bears its own costs. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition where essential language is removed, contradicting implied or explicit obligations.
